seoTitle: Build an Online Registration Form in Google Docs
metaDescription: Design a structured registration form in Google Docs using tables, dropdown chips, and checklists, complete with clean underline-style input fields.
intro: Google Docs isn't just for writing paragraphs; with its table and smart chip tools, you can build a genuinely usable registration form. This guide covers laying out a data grid, adding dropdown and checklist fields, and styling borders so the finished form looks like a proper intake sheet.

steps.heading: Set up your header and table layout
steps.body: Start with a standard registration header, then use the Insert menu to build a table that maps out attendee data fields.

steps.heading: Add dropdown chips for menu options
steps.body: Use Dropdown Chips to embed interactive selection menus directly inside table fields, such as session or ticket type.

steps.heading: Add checklists for multi-choice selections
steps.body: Insert checklist elements from the Insert menu to handle multi-choice options like meal preferences or add-ons.

steps.heading: Format borders into underline prompts
steps.body: In table properties, set specific border lines to zero points so labeled cells resemble clean underline entry fields.

steps.heading: Add background fills and check spacing
steps.body: Apply subtle cell background colors to separate input areas from labels, then verify the layout doesn't shift across margins.

faq.question: Can I add dropdown menus to a Google Docs form?
faq.answer: Yes, Dropdown Chips let you embed interactive selection menus directly inside your table cells.

faq.question: How do I make my table look like fillable lines instead of a grid?
faq.answer: Set specific border lines to zero points in table properties to create clean underline-style entry fields.

faq.question: Is this the same as a Google Form?
faq.answer: No, this builds a printable or shareable document-style registration layout, not a submission-collecting Google Form.

faq.question: Will the table stay aligned if I add more rows later?
faq.answer: Managing global table alignment and spacing settings helps keep the layout stable as you add more entries.
